issues: The short description omits the third Sockeyes-Clippers result visible in the OCR (Mike McCormick's hat-trick in an 11-6 Sockeyes win in January). There are three distinct Sockeyes game results, not two.

The long description states 'won 4-3 in a second matchup with Mike McCormick scoring the game-winner' as if this is the only other result, but the OCR also contains a third entry: Mike McCormick scoring three goals in an 11-6 Sockeyes win over Nanaimo Clippers (item 3 in the January section).
The long description says 'December 1987' but the OCR clearly covers both December 1987 and January (likely January 1988) items — this is a sports briefs roundup spanning multiple months.

Item 6 in the January section mentions Jamie Stewart winning a goaltending battle for Langley Eagles vs Nanaimo Clippers — this is a known Sockeyes-roster name, but the article refers to him playing for Langley Eagles, which is worth noting accurately.
The draft does not mention that the page is a numbered sports briefs roundup format, which is important context for understanding the clipping.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Nanaimo Daily News sports briefs roundup featuring three Richmond Sockeyes victories over Nanaimo Clippers in BCJHL action, including a Rob Clarke hat-trick in an 11-4 win, a Mike McCormick game-winner in a 4-3 win, and a McCormick hat-trick in an 11-6 win.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A numbered sports briefs column in the Nanaimo Daily News covering BCJHL and other regional sporting events across December 1987 and January 1988. Richmond Sockeyes appear three times: Rob Clarke notched a hat-trick in an 11-4 blowout of Nanaimo Clippers at Richmond Arena; Mike McCormick scored the game-winner as the Sockeyes edged Clippers 4-3 before 1,100 fans at Civic Arena; and McCormick recorded a hat-trick to lead Richmond to an 11-6 comeback win over Nanaimo before 650 fans at Civic Arena. Additional items cover Nanaimo Clippers roster cuts, the BCJHL All-Star game, basketball, soccer, and minor hockey.
review.notes: The OCR is a numbered sports briefs digest spanning both December 1987 and January (likely 1988) entries on the same page. Three separate Sockeyes game results are present (items 15, 23, and 3-January). The draft missed the third result entirely and incorrectly described the date range as solely December 1987. Also note item 6 (January) references a "Jamie Stewart" winning a goaltending battle for Langley Eagles — if this is the same Jamie Stewart on the Sockeyes roster, it may indicate a roster move; editor should verify.
